BMABMDR 0.1.20
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to BMABMDR: Bayesian Model Averaging for Benchmark Dose Analysis
  • Changelog

Basic dose-response plots for covariate analysis

Source: R/plot_model_fit.R
basic.plot.Rd

Basic dose-response plots for covariate analysis

basic.plot(x, model_name, increasing)

basic.plotQ(x, model_name)

Arguments

x

BMDBMA model object

model_name

model for which the fit should be plotted (one of get_models())

increasing

logical indicating whether the continuous data are increasing (T) or decreasing (F)

Value

a basic plot

Examples

data.test <- das1$data
ind.data <- data.frame(x = data.test$Dose, y = data.test$LDH, cov = data.test$sex)
ind.data <- ind.data[which(!is.na(ind.data$y)),]
FLBMD <- full.laplace_MA_Cov(data = ind.data, prior.weights = c(rep(1,4), rep(0,12)), sumstats = FALSE, q = 0.5, prior.d = 'N11')
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.05 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.10 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against log-normality at level 0.10 for dose 4800
#> Warning: distributional assumption of constant variance for the normal distribution is not met for group f, Bartlett test p-value is 0.0000
#> distributional assumption of constant variance are met for group m, Bartlett test p-value is 0.7033
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 18.9936 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 18.9936 
#> default prior choices used on background
#> default prior choices used on fold change
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 8.3367 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 8.3367 
#> default prior choices used on background
#> default prior choices used on fold change
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 19.5391 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 19.5391 
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 8.3284 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 8.3284 
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> default prior choices used on BMD
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.05 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.10 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against log-normality at level 0.10 for dose 4800
#> Warning: distributional assumption of constant variance for the normal distribution is not met, Bartlett test p-value is 0.0000
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> Warning: the data do not contain information on the asymptote, and the default prior for fold change has been based on 3 times the observed maximum;
#>             please provide prior input on the maximum response by specifying 'maxy', if available
#> default prior choices used on background
#> default prior choices used on fold change
#> default prior choices used on BMD
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.05 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.10 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against log-normality at level 0.10 for dose 4800
#> Warning: distributional assumption of constant variance for the normal distribution is not met for group f, Bartlett test p-value is 0.0000
#> distributional assumption of constant variance are met for group m, Bartlett test p-value is 0.7033
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 18.9936 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 18.9936 
#> default prior choices used on background
#> default prior choices used on fold change
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 8.3367 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 8.3367 
#> default prior choices used on background
#> default prior choices used on fold change
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> default prior choices used on BMD
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.05 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against normality across dose levels at level 0.10 (p-value 0.0032)
#> Warning: there is evidence against log-normality at level 0.10 for dose 4800
#> Warning: distributional assumption of constant variance for the normal distribution is not met, Bartlett test p-value is  0.0000
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 17.2104 
#> default prior choices used on background
#> default prior choices used on fold change
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 19.5391 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 19.5391 
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 1: Global Deviance = 8.3284 
#> GAMLSS-RS iteration 2: Global Deviance = 8.3284 
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> Warning: The origing of the x variable has been shifted by 1e-14
#> default prior choices used on BMD
basic.plot(FLBMD, model_name = 'E4_N', increasing = T)
